name: Flourless Chocolate Cake with Orange Liqueur
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 30 minutes. Baking time: 1 1/2 hours.
Chilling time: Several hours or overnight.
1. Place chocolate and butter in the top of a double boiler. Cook and stir over simmering water until melted. Stir in 3/4 cup of the sugar. Continue stirring until the sugar is almost dissolved.
2. Beat egg yolks in a large mixer until light. Gradually beat in some of the warm chocolate mixture until well combined. Continue adding chocolate mixture until fully combined. Return the chocolate mixture to the top of the double boiler; cook, stirring until slightly thickened. Do not boil. Remove from heat.
3. Preheat oven to 250 degrees. Butter and flour a 12-inch springform pan. Line the sides of the pan with a strip of buttered wax paper.
4. Beat egg whites in a large mixer bowl until foamy. Gradually beat in the remaining 1/2 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon at a time, until the mixture holds stiff peaks.
5. Stir liqueur and vanilla into the chocolate mixture, mixing well. With a rubber spatula, gently scrape the chocolate mixture over the egg whites. Fold together thoroughly but gently.
6. Pour batter into the prepared pan. Bake until set and top is crusty, about 1 1/2 hours. Cool on a wire rack to room temperature. Refrigerate until cold. Loosen the edge of the cake from the sides of the pan. Remove the sides of the pan. Place the cake onto a plate. Sprinkle heavily with confectioners' sugar. The top will be brittle.
